name: command_run
description: Run Codex tools as a pure batch+step command runner. Always use commands[]. Prefer one complete batch for predictable work: discover/read/search the needed context, edit, test, and validate in ordered steps when dependencies are clear. Keep related path listing, targeted search, and candidate file reads in the same batch and same read-only step when they are independent. Use early steps for needed discovery/read/search commands and later steps for apply_patch, generated writes, tests, builds, and verification that should wait for earlier results. Mutating or unknown commands act as barriers and run one at a time. Different steps run in ascending order.

commands.description: Executable command-line instructions. Do not put progress announcements or summaries here. Include the needed discovery, read, edit, test, and verification commands for the current batch.

command_type.description: Target internal Codex command name from the available commands list. For namespaced tools use the flattened name shown there exactly.

command_line.description: String payload for the target command. For JSON-schema commands, pass an escaped JSON object string such as {"cmd":"rg -n query src"}. For freeform commands such as apply_patch, pass the raw freeform body. For shell-like commands, plain text is also accepted and mapped to that tool's command field.

step.description: Execution stage. Use the same step for independent discovery/read commands and later steps for edits, tests, and validation that depend on earlier output. Commands with the same step may run concurrently when they are safe read-only work; later steps wait for earlier steps.
